question_title: Will Donald Trump's approval rating in the RealClearPolitics poll average fall strictly below 39.0% before May 1, 2026?
summary: The question asks whether the RealClearPolitics approval average for Trump will fall strictly below 39.0% at any point before May 1, 2026. As of April 3, 2026, Silver Bulletin's approval average has already dipped to 39.7%, but RCP tends to run 1-2+ points higher than Silver Bulletin due to its inclusion of Rasmussen (currently at 49%) and Harvard-Harris (49%) in a simple unweighted average. This creates a structural floor around 40-42% for RCP. For the RCP average to drop below 39%, with Rasmussen at 49% and Harvard-Harris at 49% in the mix, the remaining ~5 polls would need to average below 34-35% — an extreme scenario. The strong downward trend driven by Iran War, gas prices, tariffs, and economic pessimism provides some pressure, but the RCP methodology makes this specific threshold very difficult to reach within 28 days.

factors.description: RCP structurally higher than other averages due to inclusion of Rasmussen (49%) and Harvard-Harris (49%), creating a floor around 40-42%
RCP composition math: With Rasmussen at 49 and H-H at 49, remaining 5 polls need average below 34-35% to bring overall below 39%. Very difficult.
Status quo persistence: RCP averages are heavily smoothed and slow to move. Even in sharp declines, multi-point drops take weeks.
Strong downward trend: Silver Bulletin hit 39.7%, down from 42% in a month. All non-Rasmussen polls showing sharp declines.
Multiple negative catalysts: Iran War, gas prices at $4/gal, tariffs, economy approval at career-low 31% (CNN), Rasmussen first time without net positive
28 days remaining gives time for further decline, and any qualifying reading counts (not just endpoint)

reasoning:
**Forecast: 18% probability that the RCP approval average falls strictly below 39.0% before May 1, 2026.**

Key reasoning:
- **Silver Bulletin** has already hit 39.7%, but the question asks about **RealClearPolitics** specifically
- RCP runs 1-2+ points higher than Silver Bulletin because it includes Rasmussen (49%) and Harvard-Harris (49%) in a simple unweighted average
- This creates a structural floor: with those two pollsters in the mix, the remaining ~5 polls need to average below 34-35% to pull RCP below 39% — an extreme scenario
- Strong downward pressures exist (Iran War, $4 gas, tariff fallout, economy approval at career lows), but RCP's methodology makes this specific threshold very hard to breach
- 28 days and "any qualifying reading" provide some opportunity, but the RCP composition is the dominant constraint
sources_consulted: [Opinion polling on the second Trump presidency - Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Opinion_polling_on_the_second_Trump_presidency)
[Trump approval just hit the 30s. Can his numbers get any lower?](https://www.natesilver.net/p/trump-approval-rating-30s-popularity-decline)
[(Metaculus) Will Donald Trump's approval rating in the RealClearPolitics poll average fall strictly below 39.0% before May 1, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43045)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if the RealClearPolitics approval tracker at https://www.realclearpolling.com/polls/approval/donald-trump/approval-rating shows an approval average strictly below 39.0% at any time before May 1, 2026. Otherwise, this question resolves to no.
fine_print: Use the approval value, not net approval. If the page updates multiple times, any qualifying reading before May 1, 2026 counts.
